description: Highlight unfilled dynamic variables with yellow background. Any variable placeholder like [Nama Pihak Kedua], [Lokasi], [Nomor Kontrak] that remains unfilled in the generated HTML should be wrapped with a yellow highlight mark so users can visually identify what still needs to be filled in. This applies both to the backend HTML generation AND the frontend TipTap editor rendering.
steps: 1. Update backend/app/utils/text_processing.py: Add a new function 'highlight_unfilled_variables(html_text: str) -> str' that uses regex to find all remaining [PLACEHOLDER] patterns in the final HTML output and wraps them with <mark class="variable-highlight" style="background-color: #FFEB3B; padding: 2px 4px; border-radius: 2px;">[PLACEHOLDER]</mark>. This should be applied AFTER variable replacement so only truly unfilled ones get highlighted.

2. Update backend/app/services/drafting_service.py: After generating the final html_content (whether from LLM or direct fill), call highlight_unfilled_variables() on the result before returning the DraftResponse. Apply it in generate_draft() just before constructing the return DraftResponse.
3. Update frontend/package.json: Add @tiptap/extension-highlight dependency (version ^2.12.0) for native highlight mark support in the editor.

4. Update frontend/src/lib/components/TipTapEditor.svelte: (a) Import Highlight extension from @tiptap/extension-highlight. (b) Add Highlight.configure({ multicolor: true }) to the extensions array. (c) Add CSS in a <style> block that styles .variable-highlight marks with yellow background, ensuring the mark elements from the backend HTML render correctly in TipTap.

5. Add a global CSS rule (in frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte or a global stylesheet) that styles mark.variable-highlight and also generic <mark> tags inside .tiptap-editor with background-color: #FFEB3B to ensure consistency.

6. Update backend/tests/test_drafting.py: Add tests for highlight_unfilled_variables function - test that unfilled [Placeholder] gets wrapped with mark tag, filled variables are NOT wrapped, and nested brackets are handled correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Unfilled variables like [Nama Pihak Kedua] in generated HTML are wrapped with <mark> tags with yellow background styling
Variables that WERE filled (replaced with actual values) are NOT highlighted
The TipTap editor renders yellow highlights for mark elements in the HTML content
The highlight extension is properly installed and configured in the frontend
Tests verify the highlighting logic works correctly
verification:
cd /projects/sandbox/PengembanganAI-CLMS/backend && python -c "from app.utils.text_processing import highlight_unfilled_variables; result = highlight_unfilled_variables('

[Nama] dan [Alamat]

'); assert 'mark' in result; assert 'variable-highlight' in result; print('Highlight OK:', result)"
cd /projects/sandbox/PengembanganAI-CLMS/frontend && npm install && npm run build
